explanation: The natural language requirement (CONT.4) describes that the system transitions from Self-Test Mode to VentilationOff Mode (i.e., StandbyMode) under two conditions: when the self-test completes successfully, or when the GUI requests to resume ventilation. Since atomic propositions about explicit mode transitions are not provided, we encode this operationally as: whenever the mode is SelfTestMode and either the self-test passes or the GUI resume command occurs, the mode should transition to StandbyMode (VentilationOff Mode). The structured natural language property reflects this by stating: whenever SelfTestMode and (selfTestPass or guiResumeRequest), eventually StandbyMode. This captures both listed triggers and directly reflects the intent using the closest available atomic propositions.

explanation: This requirement describes a system transition: when the system is in SelfTestMode, it should transition to StandbyMode (which is equivalent to VentilationOff Mode) under two conditions: (1) the self-test has been completed successfully and (2) the GUI requests resuming ventilation. To encode this, we use atomic propositions provided: SelfTestMode (controller is in SelfTest Mode), selfTestPass (indicates successful self-test), and guiResumeRequest (GUI asks for resuming ventilation). The satisfaction is that whenever both these conditions (selfTestPass and guiResumeRequest) occur while in SelfTestMode, the system must immediately move to StandbyMode. We do not have an atomic proposition for 'transition to Standby Mode,' but given VentilationOff Mode is synonymous with StandbyMode, we presume StandbyMode becoming true is our postcondition. Thus, this property is structured as: while SelfTestMode, whenever (selfTestPass & guiResumeRequest), immediately satisfy StandbyMode.
